response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His database background lists MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital) is fintech/startup investing, not capital markets, investment banking, or fixed income. Most of his recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which suggests a significant retooling would be needed.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and testing tooling authorship
Some systems-programming depth via DLang and C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets / investment banking domain experience
No stated SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no Summit, Kafka/MQ, or related desirable skills

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and testing culture
